questions.question: What datasets did 'An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale' use?
questions.gold_answer: ImageNet, ImageNet-21k, CIFAR-100
questions.system_answer: The paper explicitly mentions pre-training on ImageNet-21k and JFT-300M, and it also reports transfer/evaluation results on ImageNet, ImageNet-Real, CIFAR-100, and the VTAB suite of 19 tasks. The evidence does not list any other datasets used.

questions.question: What is the parameter count of the largest variant in 'Training data-efficient image transformers & distillation through attention'?
questions.gold_answer: The largest DeiT variant is DeiT-B★ (DeiT-B with distillation) at 87M parameters.
questions.system_answer: The largest variant listed is DeiT-B★, which has 87M parameters. The paper’s reference vision transformer is 86M, but the biggest variant in the provided evidence is 87M.

questions.question: What architectural innovations does 'Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows' introduce?
questions.gold_answer: hierarchical structure, shifted window attention
questions.system_answer: The paper introduces a hierarchical vision Transformer that computes self-attention inside non-overlapping local windows, then alternates with shifted window partitioning to create cross-window connections. It also uses patch merging to build multi-scale feature maps, giving linear computational complexity with respect to image size and making it a general-purpose vision backbone. Relative position bias is another architectural choice noted as beneficial.

questions.question: Briefly describe the architecture of 'Emerging Properties in Self-Supervised Vision Transformers'.
questions.gold_answer: DINO is a self-supervised vision transformer training method
questions.system_answer: The paper presents DINO, a simple self-supervised Vision Transformer architecture based on self-distillation without labels: a student network is trained to match a momentum-teacher network across multiple augmented views, with centering and sharpening to prevent collapse. It is designed to work with both ViTs and convnets, but the paper emphasizes that ViTs with small patches benefit especially strongly.

questions.question: Do papers in this corpus agree on top-1 accuracy on ImageNet?
questions.gold_answer: No—reported top-1 ImageNet accuracy in this corpus varies widely from 1.3% to 91.0% across 594 results.
questions.system_answer: No. The corpus shows a very wide spread in reported ImageNet top-1 accuracy values, from 1.3 up to 91.0 (stddev 8.69), so the papers do not agree on a single value. There are also multiple conflicting SOTA claims, with different papers reporting best values such as 91.0 (CoCa finetuned), 90.9 (PaLI-17B), 88.7/88.51 (MaxViT-XL), and 86.5 (NFNet-F6+SAM / CaiT-M48†448†).

questions.question: What is the range of top-1 accuracy values reported on CIFAR-100 in this corpus?
questions.gold_answer: The top-1 accuracy on CIFAR-100 in this corpus ranges from 48.5% to 91.88% across 27 results.
questions.system_answer: Across the corpus, reported CIFAR-100 top-1 accuracy values range from 48.5% to 91.88%, a spread of 43.38 percentage points. The highest reported value is 91.88% (AdaptFormer-Swin), and the evidence does not list any explicit SOTA claims to compare or conflict.

questions.question: Do papers in this corpus agree on which model holds SOTA on ADE20K mIoU?
questions.gold_answer: No—this corpus does not agree on a single ADE20K mIoU SOTA model, since 7 papers each claim SOTA on the benchmark.
questions.system_answer: No. The corpus contains conflicting SOTA claims on ADE20K mIoU: InternImage-H is listed at 62.9, while ViT-Adapter-L is also claimed as SOTA at 61.5, and SwinV2-G is reported at 59.9. Based on the evidence, InternImage-H has the highest reported value, but the papers do not agree on a single SOTA model.

questions.question: Do papers in this corpus agree on mIoU values on ADE20K?
questions.gold_answer: No—reported ADE20K mIoU values vary widely across 97 results, from 26.6 to 62.9.
questions.system_answer: No. The ADE20K mIoU values in this corpus are not consistent: they range from 26.6 to 62.9, a spread of 36.3 points with stddev 7.29. There are also conflicting SOTA claims, with different papers reporting top values such as 62.9 (InternImage-H), 61.5 (ViT-Adapter-L), and 59.9 (SwinV2-G).

questions.question: Do papers in this corpus agree on whether position embeddings are necessary for vision transformers?
questions.gold_answer: Papers disagree on the necessity of position embeddings; some ablations show minimal impact while others demonstrate clear performance drops without them.
questions.system_answer: No, the corpus does not agree. Some papers treat position embeddings as part of the standard ViT design, e.g. ViT says it "adds position embeddings" and CPVT says vision transformers commonly use positional encodings, while others argue they are not necessary or can be removed with little harm: CvT says "Removing position embedding of CvT does not degrade performance," and the self-supervised ViT study says removing them "only slightly hurts performance." There is also a middle ground: several papers replace absolute embeddings with relative or conditional forms, and Swin reports absolute position embedding can even "slightly hurt dense prediction tasks."

questions.question: How did the maximum reported top-1 accuracy on ImageNet change year over year in this corpus?
questions.system_answer: The maximum reported top-1 accuracy on ImageNet increased from 85.2 in 2020 to 90.0 in 2021 and 91.0 in 2022, then declined to 87.8 in 2023 and 83.9 in 2024. Overall, the trend is up from 2020 to a peak in 2022, followed by a downward trend through 2024.
